title: Implement the approved fix
description: Apply the approved fix to setup.sh and related files so agent personalities are properly passed to Paperclip

fixProposal.breakpointQuestion: Which approach for fixing Paperclip agent personalities? Option A (recommended): volume-mount + instructionsFilePath — minimal changes, native Paperclip mechanism. Option B: CLI import — cleanest but unverified. Option C: API upload — no volume mounts but duplicates content.

cons: Requires companies/ dir at compose startup
Doesn't fix F4 (setup.sh still has hardcoded defs)
options.description: Mount companies/go-north/agents into Paperclip container, set instructionsFilePath in adapterConfig per agent. Paperclip reads AGENTS.md at runtime via its native instructions bundle system.
pros: AGENTS.md stays single source of truth
options.title: Volume-mount + instructionsFilePath

cons: CLI command unverified — may not exist yet
Highest risk — depends on unverified functionality
options.description: Replace manual registration with single 'paperclip import companies/go-north' CLI call. Discovers AGENTS.md by convention, applies .paperclip.yaml.
pros: .paperclip.yaml becomes true single source of truth
options.title: Paperclip company import CLI

cons: setup.sh becomes complex (YAML parsing, multi-step API)
Edits to AGENTS.md require re-running setup
options.description: Read AGENTS.md content during setup, push via Paperclip API (capabilities field + instructions bundle upload). No volume mounts needed.
options.title: API upload at setup time

flaws.rootCause: AGENT_DEFS hardcodes only name/role/title/model, never reads AGENTS.md files
flaws.title: setup.sh sends zero personality content in agent registration

flaws.impact: Claude adapter cannot inject personality via --append-system-prompt-file
flaws.rootCause: adapterConfig contains only model and dangerouslySkipPermissions, not file paths
flaws.title: instructionsFilePath/instructionsRootPath not set

flaws.impact: All agents get identical generic boilerplate
flaws.rootCause: Paperclip generates generic default when no instructionsFilePath configured
flaws.title: Auto-materialized default AGENTS.md overwrites custom personality

flaws.impact: Two disconnected sources of truth, silent config drift
flaws.rootCause: setup.sh hardcodes values, never reads the YAML file
flaws.title: .paperclip.yaml declared but never consumed by setup.sh

flaws.impact: No capability-based routing or discovery in Paperclip
flaws.rootCause: API supports capabilities field, setup.sh never populates it
flaws.title: capabilities field unused in agent registration

flaws.impact: No delegation chains or escalation paths
flaws.rootCause: AGENTS.md defines reportsTo but setup.sh doesn't set it
flaws.title: reportsTo field unused — org chart not established

flaws.impact: Even with instructionsFilePath set, files would be inaccessible inside container
flaws.rootCause: docker-compose.yml mounts only paperclip-data volume, not company agent files
flaws.title: AGENTS.md files not volume-mounted into Paperclip container

flaws.impact: Declarative config cannot drive personality delivery
flaws.rootCause: YAML defines only adapter_type and model, no instructions/capabilities fields
flaws.title: .paperclip.yaml lacks personality configuration fields

flawsReport.summary: 8 distinct flaws cause agents to lose 100% of their personality. Root problem: multi-layered disconnect — setup.sh hardcodes bare metadata without reading personality files, .paperclip.yaml is declared but never consumed, Paperclip API personality mechanisms (instructionsFilePath, capabilities, reportsTo) are unused, Docker image doesn't mount AGENTS.md files, and auto-materialization overwrites custom personalities with generic defaults.

agents.gap: AGENTS.md defines Go-North PM with backlog management, user story structure rules, Hebrew-first copy, stakeholder translation, sprint planning, and cross-agent coordination. API payload sends only name/role/model.
AGENTS.md defines budget tracker with USD+ILS reporting, LLM token cost analysis, $0.50 threshold, weekly cost ledger. API payload sends only name/role/model.
AGENTS.md defines Next.js/React/Tailwind developer with RTL support, mobile-first, UX review gate. API payload sends only name/role/model.
AGENTS.md defines Supabase/Postgres developer with RLS policies, centralized prompt registry, LLM call logging. API payload sends only name/role/model.
AGENTS.md defines QA gatekeeper with deploy sign-off, Playwright E2E, visual regression, real mobile viewport testing. API payload sends only name/role/model.
AGENTS.md defines Figma-native designer with mobile-first mandate, real Hebrew content, single Figma source of truth. API payload sends only name/role/model.

validationReport.summary: All 6 AGENTS.md files contain rich, unique personality definitions (role context, capabilities, behavior rules, tech stack, collaboration requirements) specific to Go-North. However, setup.sh sends only structural metadata (name, role, title, adapterType, model) to the Paperclip API -- zero personality content is included. The Paperclip API fully supports injecting personality via adapterConfig.instructionsFilePath, adapterConfig.instructionsRootPath, or the capabilities field, but setup.sh uses none of these. When agents are created without instructions config, Paperclip auto-generates a generic default AGENTS.md from built-in onboarding assets, not from the project's custom files. The .paperclip.yaml file also lacks instructions configuration and is not consumed by setup.sh at all.
